topics: 入职新公司进展（威高长三角）
集采政策与行业政策区别
医疗器械产品描述练习（手术机器人）

grammarErrors.incorrect: It was been good
grammarErrors.correct: I've been good
grammarErrors.example: How have you been? — I've been good, thanks.

grammarErrors.incorrect: Yes, I have read the read the parent
grammarErrors.correct: I have read the parent company's report
grammarErrors.example: I have read the financial report already.

grammarErrors.incorrect: policy that will infect the company
grammarErrors.correct: policy that will affect the company
grammarErrors.example: New regulations will affect our pricing strategy.

grammarErrors.incorrect: procoment / procoment
grammarErrors.correct: procurement
grammarErrors.example: Centralized procurement has reduced costs significantly.

business.example: The centralized procurement policy has reduced stent prices by 90%.
Industry policy changes have a bigger impact than government regulations.
Government policy affects all companies in the market.
The new policy will affect our revenue significantly.
I have read the parent company's financial report.

medical.example: 90% of hospitals in China have used this surgical robot.
If you buy in large quantities, you'll receive a bulk discount.

notes.title: Present Perfect基础构成：have/has + 过去分词
notes.content: 永远不能说 'was been' 或 'is been'。have/has 后面直接跟过去分词。
examples: ✓ I've been good
✓ I have read the report
✗ It was been good
✗ I have been read the report

notes.title: affect vs infect
notes.content: affect = 影响（中性词）；infect = 感染（医学/负面）
examples: ✓ The policy will affect the company
✓ The virus infected 100 people
✗ The policy will infect the company

improvements: 能够讨论复杂的行业政策话题
主动查词并尝试使用（如centralized procurement）
产品介绍练习中能够说出价格、市场份额等关键信息

topics: 美式vs英式发音区别（vase, mop, cookie）

grammarErrors.incorrect: half past nine / at nine half
grammarErrors.correct: half past nine (✓) / nine thirty
grammarErrors.errorType: 时间表达——half past是正确的
grammarErrors.example: I got up at half past nine this morning.

grammarErrors.incorrect: he snaps sleep at two am
grammarErrors.correct: he sleeps at two am
grammarErrors.example: He often sleeps very late because he plays games.

grammarErrors.incorrect: the teacher will give you less school
grammarErrors.correct: the teacher will deduct marks / lower your grade
grammarErrors.example: If you skip class, the teacher will deduct marks from your grade.

grammarErrors.incorrect: 70 percent is from your exam
grammarErrors.correct: 70 percent of your grade comes from the exam
grammarErrors.example: 70% of your final grade comes from the written exam.

pronunciation.correct: 两种都可以接受，美式更常用

business.example: The teacher will deduct marks if you miss class.
Your attendance affects your final grade.
He earns money from streaming games online.

notes.title: 美式英语 vs 英式英语——常见区别
notes.content: 对于职场英语，学美式即可。不需要专门学英式。
examples: cookie（美）= biscuit（英）
mop（美）= mop（英，发音不同）
vase /veɪz/（美）= vase /vɑːz/（英）

notes.title: sp/st发音：p和t不发成b和d
notes.content: 中国学生常把sp发成sb，st发成sd。虽然听感差不多，但正式场合应该发p和t。
examples: spell → /spel/，不是/sbel/
stop → /stɒp/，不是/sdɒp/
但这个错误通常不影响理解

improvements: 能用英语自然讨论日常生活话题
能比较不同国家的教育制度
主动表达观点（'I think it's ok because...'）
nextFocus: 继续扩展词汇（特别是动词搭配）
减少说话时的重复启动

topics: 美式vs英式发音深入讨论
医疗器械词汇：ultrasound, anesthesia, pulse oximeter等

grammarErrors.incorrect: for four fours
grammarErrors.correct: for four hours
grammarErrors.example: We climbed the mountain for four hours.

grammarErrors.incorrect: I was too tired to go so I rest for yesterday
grammarErrors.correct: I was too tired, so I rested yesterday
grammarErrors.example: I was exhausted, so I rested the whole day.

grammarErrors.incorrect: if I do the exercise at night and my heart will
grammarErrors.correct: if I exercise at night, my heart rate goes up and I can't sleep
grammarErrors.example: If I exercise late at night, I can't fall asleep easily.

grammarErrors.incorrect: there is not much people
grammarErrors.correct: there aren't many people
grammarErrors.example: There aren't many people at the gym before 4pm.

business.example: A CFO in a startup often handles multiple roles.
Making a career change from auditor to CFO is challenging but rewarding.

medical.example: The ultrasound machine is used to check internal organs.
General anesthesia is required for major surgeries.
A pulse oximeter measures blood oxygen levels.
Check for a pulse by pressing two fingers on the wrist.
A defibrillator is used to restore normal heart rhythm.
Nebulizers are used to deliver medication directly to the lungs.

notes.title: many vs much — 可数与不可数
examples: ✓ there aren't many people
✓ there isn't much time
✗ there isn't much people
✓ not many hospitals / not much equipment

notes.title: 条件句：if + 一般现在时，主句用will/现在时
notes.content: 真实条件句中，if从句用一般现在时，不用will
examples: ✓ If I exercise at night, I can't sleep
✗ If I will exercise at night, I can't sleep
✓ If you buy in bulk, we offer a discount

improvements: 能够用英语描述医疗器械的功能
产品介绍越来越自然流利
能讨论日常健康话题

topics: 威高长三角平台三大业务方向
AI工具使用（Claude Code搭建学习系统）

grammarErrors.incorrect: at Friday
grammarErrors.correct: on Friday
grammarErrors.example: I will ask HR on Friday morning.

grammarErrors.incorrect: I will ask, ask the Hr tomorrow or the or on at at Friday
grammarErrors.correct: I'll ask HR tomorrow or on Friday
grammarErrors.example: I'll check with HR on Friday.

grammarErrors.incorrect: innovation equation
grammarErrors.correct: innovation incubation
grammarErrors.example: The platform focuses on innovation incubation for healthcare startups.

grammarErrors.incorrect: I will responsible
grammarErrors.correct: I will be responsible
grammarErrors.example: The CFO will be responsible for all financial decisions.

business.example: The platform specializes in medical-industrial collaboration between hospitals and companies.
Innovation incubation helps startups develop new medical technologies.
Our third focus is investment in emerging healthcare companies.
As CFO, I am responsible for all financial reporting.
on Monday / on Friday / on weekends — never 'at Friday'

notes.title: 介词三规则：on / in / at
notes.content: 这是本节课Maxim专门停下来纠正的核心语法点
examples: on + 星期/日期：on Friday, on March 16th
in + 月份/年份/时间段：in March, in 2024, in the morning
at + 具体时刻/地点：at 8 o'clock, at home, at the office
✗ 永远不能说 'at Friday'

notes.title: be responsible for — 不能省略be
notes.content: 这个固定搭配必须有be动词
examples: ✓ I will be responsible for the report
✓ She is responsible for the team
✗ I will responsible for the report

improvements: 能够用英语解释公司三大业务方向
主动用AI工具辅助学习
能够把中文业务概念转化为英语表达

topics: 入职进展（老板还没回来）
财政部高端会计人才培训班
医疗器械词汇：ultrasound, anesthesia, syringe等

grammarErrors.incorrect: It's organized by the Ministry of Finance — subsidiaries
grammarErrors.correct: subsidy（补贴）vs subsidiary（子公司）
grammarErrors.example: The government provides a subsidy to support small businesses.

grammarErrors.incorrect: maybe a few days later when I will go
grammarErrors.correct: a few days later, when I go / when he comes back
grammarErrors.errorType: 时间状语从句不用将来时
grammarErrors.example: I'll start work when my boss comes back.

grammarErrors.incorrect: from nine o'clock to Saturday
grammarErrors.correct: from Friday to Sunday / from 9am to 5pm
grammarErrors.example: The class runs from Friday to Sunday, 9am to 5pm each day.

business.example: The company received a government subsidy for R&D investment.
The Ministry of Finance organizes training for senior accounting professionals.
subsidy = 补贴（政府给的钱）；subsidiary = 子公司（完全不同！）
The class covers macro policy and its impact on small businesses.

medical.example: The IV drip stand allows patients to move while receiving fluids.

notes.title: subsidy vs subsidiary — 完全不同的词
notes.content: 这两个词读音相似但意思完全不同，是高频混淆词
examples: subsidy /ˈsʌbsɪdi/ = 补贴、资助（政府给企业或个人的钱）
subsidiary /səbˈsɪdiəri/ = 子公司（母公司控股的公司）
✓ government subsidy（政府补贴）
✓ wholly-owned subsidiary（全资子公司）

notes.title: when引导的时间从句——不用will
notes.content: when / after / before / as soon as 引导的时间状语从句，用一般现在时
examples: ✓ I'll call you when I arrive
✓ when my boss comes back
✗ when I will go
✗ when my boss will come back

improvements: 能够解释复杂的培训项目内容
医疗器械词汇积累越来越多
能够区分相似词汇（主动询问subsidy vs subsidiary）

topics: 用Claude Code搭建行业英语学习系统
骨科产品词汇：intramedullary nails, screws and plates, titanium

grammarErrors.incorrect: I successfully install
grammarErrors.correct: I have successfully installed / I successfully installed
grammarErrors.errorType: 动词时态——已完成的动作
grammarErrors.example: I have successfully installed the Claude Code tool.

grammarErrors.incorrect: I am doing the doing the work now / I'm doing the doing
grammarErrors.correct: I am installing / I have been building
grammarErrors.example: I have been building this learning system for a few days.

grammarErrors.incorrect: the cloud code is helping me to build this system
grammarErrors.correct: Claude Code is helping me build this system
grammarErrors.example: Claude helped me build the vocabulary system.

business.example: I pay for tokens to use the Claude API for my learning system.
I have successfully installed the system on my laptop.

medical.example: Intramedullary nails are inserted into the bone canal to stabilize fractures.
Screws and plates made of titanium are used to fix bone fractures.
Orthopedic implants are made of titanium because it is biocompatible.
Trauma fixation devices stabilize broken bones during healing.

notes.title: help后面用不带to的不定式
notes.content: help somebody do something（不是help somebody to do something，虽然两者都可用，但前者更自然）
examples: ✓ Claude helped me build the system
✓ Claude helped me to build the system（也可以）
✓ This tool helps me learn faster

notes.title: Present Perfect vs Simple Past
notes.content: 强调动作的结果/完成状态用PP；强调动作发生的时间用Simple Past
examples: ✓ I have installed it（强调：装好了，现在可以用）
✓ I installed it yesterday（强调：昨天这件事发生了）
✗ I successfully install（时态完全错误）

improvements: 能够演示和解释自己搭建的技术系统
医疗器械词汇覆盖到了骨科植入物
主动使用AI工具辅助学习，方法先进
nextFocus: PP vs Simple Past区分练习
减少口头重复（做到不重复启动）

topics: 美国市场vs中国市场（market vs policy market）

grammarErrors.incorrect: He came back. He comes back this week
grammarErrors.correct: He will come back this week / He is coming back this week
grammarErrors.example: I think he will come back this week.

grammarErrors.incorrect: the government will net the company to be a public company
grammarErrors.correct: the government will allow / approve the company to go public
grammarErrors.example: The CSRC must approve a company before it can go public.

grammarErrors.incorrect: gave some gives some money
grammarErrors.correct: gave some money / bribed officials
grammarErrors.example: Some companies allegedly bribed CSRC officials to get listed.

grammarErrors.incorrect: one day people will know
grammarErrors.correct: one day the truth will come out
grammarErrors.example: You can't hide it forever — the truth will come out eventually.

business.example: The CSRC must approve a company's IPO application before listing.
The company plans to go public on the Shanghai Stock Exchange next year.
China's stock market is often described as a policy-driven market.
You can't cover up fraud forever — the truth will always come out.
Several CSRC officials were arrested for taking bribes from IPO applicants.

medical.example: This is a coronary stent — a tiny metal tool that holds a blocked artery open.
Next-generation bioabsorbable stents dissolve naturally in the body.
Weigao was a pioneer in China's first drug-coated stent in 2500.

medical.english: centralized procurement cut the price by 93%
medical.example: Centralized procurement cut the coronary stent price from 10,000 to 700 RMB.

notes.title: go public vs get listed — 两种说法都常用
examples: go public = 公司首次公开发行股票
get listed = 在证券交易所挂牌上市
IPO = Initial Public Offering（首次公开募股）
✓ The company plans to go public next year
✓ We need to get listed on the Shanghai Exchange

notes.title: 集采政策核心表达
notes.content: 你能用英文清楚解释这个政策了，继续练习数字和比例
examples: centralized procurement = 集中带量采购
cut the price by X% = 将价格降低X%
the government purchases directly = 政府直接采购
reduce the middlemen = 减少中间商

improvements: 能够用英文完整解释集采政策（含数字）
能够解释IPO流程和监管机构
主动尝试说生词（bioabsorbable, regulatory等）
能够进行较长的连续英语表达
nextFocus: 继续深入医疗器械介入产品词汇
